meaning: adj.敏锐的,凛冽的,轻快的,活泼的vt.使活泼vi.活跃起来
examples: A brisk walk should blow the cobwebs away.
As they started up again, the horse broke into a brisk trot.
She adopted a brisk businesslike tone.
It does a brisk trade in cappuccinos and cafe lattes, and is perfect for a mid-morning pick-me up.
Dr Biddle said 30 minutes of brisk walking each day would bring substantial health benefits.
